titles.ar: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ فِيهَا
titles.en: Chapter 78 – The Masjids which are correct for the Itikaaf to be therein

titles.ar: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ فِيهَا
titles.en: Chapter 78 – The Masjids which are correct for the Itikaaf to be therein

segments: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا تَقُولُ فِي الِاعْتِكَافِ بِبَغْدَادَ فِي بَعْضِ مَسَاجِدِهَا فَقَالَ لَا اعْتِكَافَ إِلَّا فِي مَسْجِدِ جَمَاعَةٍ قَدْ صَلَّى فِيهِ إِمَامٌ عَدْلٌ بِصَلَاةِ جَمَاعَةٍ وَ لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُعْتَكَفَ فِي مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ وَ الْبَصْرَةِ وَ مَسْجِدِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَ مَسْجِدِ مَكَّةَ .
arabic.text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا تَقُولُ فِي الِاعْتِكَافِ بِبَغْدَادَ فِي بَعْضِ مَسَاجِدِهَا فَقَالَ لَا اعْتِكَافَ إِلَّا فِي مَسْجِدِ جَمَاعَةٍ قَدْ صَلَّى فِيهِ إِمَامٌ عَدْلٌ بِصَلَاةِ جَمَاعَةٍ وَ لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُعْتَكَفَ فِي مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ وَ الْبَصْرَةِ وَ مَسْجِدِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَ مَسْجِدِ مَكَّةَ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Umar Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘What are youasws saying regarding the Itikaaf at Baghdad in some of its Masjids?’ So heasws said: ‘There is no Itikaaf except in a general Masjid in which a just Imamasws has Prayed the congregational Prayer, and there is no problem if one was to do Itikaaf in Masjid of Al-Kufa, and Al-Basra, and Masjid of Al-Medina, and Masjid of Makkah’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Umar Bin Yazeed who said, ‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘What are youasws saying regarding the Itikaaf at Baghdad in some of its Masjids?’ So heasws said: ‘There is no Itikaaf except in a general Masjid in which a just Imamasws has Prayed the congregational Prayer, and there is no problem if one was to do Itikaaf in Masjid of Al-Kufa, and Al-Basra, and Masjid of Al-Medina, and Masjid of Makkah’.

segments: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from ‘Umar ibn Yazid who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about performing Ttikaf in a certain Masjid of Baghdad. He (the Imam) said, ’Without a Masjid in which Salat (prayer) in congregation is held behind a just Imam, Ttikaf is not valid. It is not harmful to do Ttikaf in Masjid of al-Kufah, in al- Basrah, in al-Madinah or Makkah.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from ‘Umar ibn Yazid who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about performing Ttikaf in a certain Masjid of Baghdad. He (the Imam) said, ’Without a Masjid in which Salat (prayer) in congregation is held behind a just Imam, Ttikaf is not valid. It is not harmful to do Ttikaf in Masjid of al-Kufah, in al- Basrah, in al-Madinah or Makkah.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/428)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/428)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ قَالَ

titles.ar: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ فِيهَا
titles.en: Chapter 78 – The Masjids which are correct for the Itikaaf to be therein

segments: لَا اعْتِكَافَ إِلَّا فِي الْعِشْرِينَ مِنْ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ وَ قَالَ إِنَّ عَلِيّاً صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ كَانَ يَقُولُ لَا أَرَى الِاعْتِكَافَ إِلَّا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَوْ مَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ أَوْ مَسْجِدٍ جَامِعٍ وَ لَا يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُعْتَكِفِ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ إِلَّا لِحَاجَةٍ لَا بُدَّ مِنْهَا ثُمَّ لَا يَجْلِسْ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ وَ الْمَرْأَةُ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ .
arabic.text: لَا اعْتِكَافَ إِلَّا فِي الْعِشْرِينَ مِنْ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ وَ قَالَ إِنَّ عَلِيّاً صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ كَانَ يَقُولُ لَا أَرَى الِاعْتِكَافَ إِلَّا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَوْ مَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ أَوْ مَسْجِدٍ جَامِعٍ وَ لَا يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُعْتَكِفِ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ إِلَّا لِحَاجَةٍ لَا بُدَّ مِنْهَا ثُمَّ لَا يَجْلِسْ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ وَ الْمَرْأَةُ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ .

segments: Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Dawood Bin Sirham,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is no Itikaaf except during the twentieth of a Month of Ramazan’. And heasws said: ‘Aliasws was saying: ‘Iasws do not view the Itikaaf (as correct) except in the Sacred Masjid, or Masjid of the Rasoolsaww, or a general Masjid. And, it is not befitting for the performer of Itikaaf that he goes out from the Masjid except for a need which is a must from it. Then he should not sit until he returns. And (for) the woman, is similar to that’.
en.hubeali.text: Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Dawood Bin Sirham, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is no Itikaaf except during the twentieth of a Month of Ramazan’. And heasws said: ‘Aliasws was saying: ‘Iasws do not view the Itikaaf (as correct) except in the Sacred Masjid, or Masjid of the Rasoolsaww, or a general Masjid. And, it is not befitting for the performer of Itikaaf that he goes out from the Masjid except for a need which is a must from it. Then he should not sit until he returns. And (for) the woman, is similar to that’.

segments: 2. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Without passing the twentieth day of the month of Ramadan Ttikaf is not valid.’ He (the Imam) then said that Ali ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say, “I do not see Ttikaf, unless it is in Masjid al-Haram, Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, or a Jami‘ Masjid (central). It is not proper for one performing ’Ttikaf to go out of Masjid except for one’s urgent needs without which one cannot do, but then one must not sit down until one returns. The same rule applies to women.’””
en.sarwar.text: 2. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Without passing the twentieth day of the month of Ramadan Ttikaf is not valid.’ He (the Imam) then said that Ali ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say, “I do not see Ttikaf, unless it is in Masjid al-Haram, Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, or a Jami‘ Masjid (central). It is not proper for one performing ’Ttikaf to go out of Masjid except for one’s urgent needs without which one cannot do, but then one must not sit down until one returns. The same rule applies to women.’””

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/429)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/429)

narratorChain.raw: سَهْلُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ سِرْحَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ فِيهَا
titles.en: Chapter 78 – The Masjids which are correct for the Itikaaf to be therein

segments: سُئِلَ عَنِ الِاعْتِكَافِ قَالَ لَا يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ إِلَّا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَوْ مَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَوْ مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ أَوْ مَسْجِدِ جَمَاعَةٍ وَ تَصُومُ مَا دُمْتَ مُعْتَكِفاً .
arabic.text: سُئِلَ عَنِ الِاعْتِكَافِ قَالَ لَا يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ إِلَّا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَوْ مَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَوْ مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ أَوْ مَسْجِدِ جَمَاعَةٍ وَ تَصُومُ مَا دُمْتَ مُعْتَكِفاً .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘Heasws was asked about the Itikaaf. Heasws said: ‘The Itikaaf is not correct except in the Sacred Masjid, or Masjid of the Rasoolsaww, or Masjid of Al-Kufa, or a general Masjid, and you should be Fasting for as long as you are in Itikaaf’.
en.hubeali.text: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘Heasws was asked about the Itikaaf. Heasws said: ‘The Itikaaf is not correct except in the Sacred Masjid, or Masjid of the Rasoolsaww, or Masjid of Al-Kufa, or a general Masjid, and you should be Fasting for as long as you are in Itikaaf’.

segments: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about ’Ttikaf. He (the Imam) said, ‘Without Masjid ’Ttikaf is not valid, only in Masjid like Masjid al-Haram, Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, Masjid al-Kufah or Masjid al-Jami‘ and you must fast in the time you are in the state of ’Ttikaf.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about ’Ttikaf. He (the Imam) said, ‘Without Masjid ’Ttikaf is not valid, only in Masjid like Masjid al-Haram, Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, Masjid al-Kufah or Masjid al-Jami‘ and you must fast in the time you are in the state of ’Ttikaf.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/430)
gradings.label: حسن
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/430)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ حَمَّادٍ عَنِ الْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ فِيهَا
titles.en: Chapter 78 – The Masjids which are correct for the Itikaaf to be therein

segments: الْمُعْتَكِفُ بِمَكَّةَ يُصَلِّي فِي أَيِّ بُيُوتِهَا شَاءَ سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ صَلَّى أَوْ فِي بُيُوتِهَا .
arabic.text: الْمُعْتَكِفُ بِمَكَّةَ يُصَلِّي فِي أَيِّ بُيُوتِهَا شَاءَ سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ صَلَّى أَوْ فِي بُيُوتِهَا .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said,
‘The one performing Itikaaf in Makkah can Pray in whichever of its house he so desires to. It is the same to him whether he Prays in the Masjid or he Prays in its houses’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said, ‘The one performing Itikaaf in Makkah can Pray in whichever of its house he so desires to. It is the same to him whether he Prays in the Masjid or he Prays in its houses’.

segments: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One performing ’Ttikaf in Makkah can perform Salat (prayer) in any of its houses or Masjid; it is all the same.’”
en.sarwar.text: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One performing ’Ttikaf in Makkah can perform Salat (prayer) in any of its houses or Masjid; it is all the same.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/430)
gradings.label: صحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/430)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ

titles.ar: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي يَصْلُحُ الِاعْتِكَافُ فِيهَا
titles.en: Chapter 78 – The Masjids which are correct for the Itikaaf to be therein

segments: الْمُعْتَكِفُ بِمَكَّةَ يُصَلِّي فِي أَيِّ بُيُوتِهَا شَاءَ وَ الْمُعْتَكِفُ فِي غَيْرِهِ لَا يُصَلِّي إِلَّا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الَّذِي سَمَّاهُ .
arabic.text: الْمُعْتَكِفُ بِمَكَّةَ يُصَلِّي فِي أَيِّ بُيُوتِهَا شَاءَ وَ الْمُعْتَكِفُ فِي غَيْرِهِ لَا يُصَلِّي إِلَّا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الَّذِي سَمَّاهُ .

segments: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one performing Itikaaf in Makkah can Pray in whichever of its house he so desires to, and the one performing Itikaaf somewhere else cannot Pray except in the Masjid which he has specified’.
en.hubeali.text: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Mansour Bin Hazim, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one performing Itikaaf in Makkah can Pray in whichever of its house he so desires to, and the one performing Itikaaf somewhere else cannot Pray except in the Masjid which he has specified’.

segments: 5. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan ibn Yahya from Mansur ibn Hazim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One who performs ’Ttikaf in Makkah can perform Salat (prayer) in any of its houses that he chooses, and one performing ’Ttikaf in other places cannot perform Salat (prayer), unless it is the Masjid which he has determined.’”
en.sarwar.text: 5. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan ibn Yahya from Mansur ibn Hazim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One who performs ’Ttikaf in Makkah can perform Salat (prayer) in any of its houses that he chooses, and one performing ’Ttikaf in other places cannot perform Salat (prayer), unless it is the Masjid which he has determined.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/430)
gradings.label: صحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/430)

narratorChain.raw: أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الْأَشْعَرِيُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ
